author: jsign
content:
We are soon moving away from `witness-generator-cli` and rely on EEST fixtures also for benchmarks. The idea is that `zkevm-releases@...` have both tests and benchmarks (the latter filled with geth t8n).

That will provide a single format for everything and we can avoid this extra `witenss-generator-cli` tool since everything will be in the `statelessInputBytes` and `statelessOutputBytes` in the EEST fixtures.

For "live networks" `statelessInputBytes` generation, I would recomend using `witness-generator-spec-cli` which is a separate and simpler tool I created a week ago that uses a CL and EL RPCs to create the `statelessInputBytes` for a live network.

LMK if htat makes sense to you.

author: 0xrubo
content: makes sense. you mean the exact json files for tests are gonna be used for benchmarks?

author: 0xrubo
content: so are files from v0.4.1 usable for benchmarks?

author: jsign
content:
Benchmarks in EEST are just special tests files. The v0.4.1 release (or v0.5.0) doesn't yet have the benchmark fixtures.
But see this thread https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1451216716062523507/1517623278397685940 and mostly in the end you ahve a v0.4.1 version of benchmarks filled with Geth that Omer validated as green.
That's last .tar I shared that he validated is exactly what we are talking about here

author: 0xrubo
content: oh right, i forgot about it. so these files are generated with `witness-generator-spec-cli`?

author: jsign
content:
Nop -- those files are created with EEST by using a special flag to fill the tests that allows to use an external t8n tool instead of the EEST Python one.
SO I used Geth to generate the execution witness for the EEST tests -- no `zkevm-benchmark-workload` repo involved for all that thread.

author: jsign
content: This `witness-generator-spec-cli` is only for creating `statelessInputBytes` for live networks by calling CL and EL RPCs.

author: jsign
content: My idea is to make this filling part of execution-specs CI release creation, so those fixtures are also part of releases.

author: jsign
content: I use that CLI only for the R2 bucket thinkg of `glamsterdam-devnet-5` stuff you saw the other day.

author: jsign
content: Lmk if this clarifies and connects all dots :)
